item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, this set me onto some more Fifthist stuff and I now get what you were going for way better than I previously did. My previous issue with this being a very promising beginning which just kind of tapers off still stands but I'm going to no vote rather than -1.
This starts off as a very solid premise for a higher dimension but ends far too quickly and disappointingly in order to direct readers to another document and some vague points about Fifthism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article… sorry… I'm on the fence. On the one hands, this idea is pretty well, and this story is told excellently. On the other hand, I was disappointed at the ending of it. Maybe I haven't understood it, I just feel that is a story about O5 being brainwashed by fifthist…

Do not know if want. Downvoted for the time being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is some issue with image corousel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I got 3125 vibes the whole way thru this and yelled "I knew it" when I saw an antimemetics crosslink.

I'll say I really liked parts of this and I can tell y'all worked hard on it. I think it could be really amazing with a little kneading. :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry but I do not think this should get the slot for 5555.

Unfortunately, this is the only fifthist entry in the 5000 contest which makes me really sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reduction of Fifthism into a Silent Hill cult is enough for a downvote, but I wasn't impressed by the aimless technobabble either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that last bit is very… uh, okay. But the rest is fine, I guess?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sorry, but i gotta agree with the other commenters here. that last link completely derails an amazing and fantastic buildup. yet i feel like the ending is a key part of the overall story here, which is even more disappointing. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are definitely the bones of a good skip under all of this, but that GitHub link at the end really kills this for me hard. The flashing and the sounds are distracting, unnecessary, and potentially dangerous to epileptic users, and forcing me to go off site to get the whole story for your article is just a straight up bad practice. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i was complaining on the scpd discord about how 5kon was going to only be people trying to make fifthist works for the 5555 slot

surprised this was the only one, and i'm happy it is. 3125 was one of my favorite articles for a long while, so this was interesting to read through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just finished it, bridges the gap between 3125 and fifthism as a whole quite well. And the little end thing was neat.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really a fan of this. I don't feel it's interesting on its own and it doesn't quite feel like a "grand unified theory"/origin story/whatever of fifthism either (especially considering the 5555 slot). gee0765 above mentioned most of my problems with this entry in their critique, but I also seriously disliked the technobabble. Usually I don't mind it (in reasonable quantities), but it just makes no sense here and ruins my suspension of disbelief.

math nitpicks
– hide block

Diagrams of SCP-5000's aperture depicted as a 5-polytope manifold;

I have absolutely no idea what a "5-polytope manifold" is. Since both google and arxiv give no results, I assume it's not a real thing (please correct me if I'm wrong). I suggest removing it and mentioning a Petrie polygon/projection of the polytope onto the Coxeter plane instead, since that's what the pictures are depicting.

Also, it seems the first image actually corresponds to Schlafli symbol h{4,3,3,3}; I suggest making a correction so that the image and text are in agreement.

envelop or dominate weaker ideatic concepts in order to increase in fractal topology.

Again, no idea what "to increase in fractal topology" means. I know what increasing a number (such as a fractal dimension) is; I also know what comparing topologies is, but that's probably not something you'd want to include in the article.

a uniform 5-polytope (Schläfli symbol t1{3,3,3,4})

Dropped the subscript code here.

I also found the two paragraphs of the researcher trying to explain something about cardinal numbers out-of-place (the scip mentions the "size" of the entities several times, but doesn't go anywhere with this concept; it also seems weird to me to try to explain one mathematical concept but not the others), but maybe that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: bro I like this a lot but PLEASE make that epilepsy warning more obvious. Withholding an otherwise enthusiastic +1 until then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, it's not obvious to anyone why you don't have time to read it, but this is more to do with where it came from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Epic. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I obviously don’t have time to read the whole thing but from my initial impressions this just seems to be the science behind 3125?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the draft, like the finished product!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this a lot, but I'm gonna have to downvote due to a disappointing and anti-climactic ending. :( I had lots of fun 'Oh shit!' moments while reading it, but none of them really panned out into anything truly epic. There's a lot of potential with O5-5, and I really wish it had been capitalized on more meaningfully. It honestly would have been just no-vote, but the link at the end was disappointing enough for me to downvote.

Still, thank you for writing this! With a bit more work, I'm sure it can be something really fun. Here's hoping you get the -5555 slot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion]
– hide block

I didn't enjoy this entry for a fair few reasons. The buildup is unmemorable, with the only thing really standing out being the attempt to "explain" some other anomalies (that's not the best word but you get the gist). However, it doesn't really explain them, more claims that their behaviour suggests the existence of this dimension without any real reason why. The Petrislav notes lean a little too much into conceptual absurdity a little too early, leaving the following section with much less impact of any form.

I don't understand why the researcher's notes are in the format they're in, and not just a part of the description. It feels more like an attempt to fit in an author avatar than anything else; it would be much easier for someone to quickly get to grips with the document if the description-related information was concise and all in one place. While some of the information isn't something they're 100% sure of, the use of hypothetical in the first line of the description shows that that's not a dealbreaker in this file.

The ending (not the linked document) is disappointing and not impactful for me. The initial description isn't the issue, though I feel that this whole conceptual/dimensional angle goes too unexplained and makes me try to join dots that aren't there. The ritual isn't unique in any way, and it made me realise that the explanation for O5-5 ending up in this mindset is quite weak. There's little justification behind his actions in the previous mission beyond his number being five. The final interview felt awfully similar to what I expect from fifthism with little to deviate from the status quo. It's just the O5, who's clearly been brainwashed, talking about how he wants to bring something godlike in the fifthist dimension to our dimension. Mind-shattering deity-like beings have been done in a better way by SCP-3125 and its surrounding works, as the incredible characterisation and surrounding anomalies/articles justify the not especially unique premise. This doesn't do that for me.

I would already likely have downvoted before I clicked that final link due to the reasons above, but all it did was solidify my vote. Even with the warning, unpausable animations are extremely hostile to the reading experience, and on a site for reading I'm not sure that's what I want. The final event is extremely cliché too, a generic descent into spam of "fifth" is one of my least favourite fifthist tropes.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Abide with me, fast falls the eventide
The darkness deepens Lord, with me abide
When other helpers fail and comforts flee
Help of the helpless, oh, abide with me

Thanks to Floppyphoenix and all the other critters for reviewing this. This contest took quite a bit from the both of us but we pulled through! Seagull was an amazing collab partner throughout the whole thing!

Also, I'm surprised that this is the only Fifthist-related entry for the contest :P

Oh well, I'm going to bed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally, a Fifthist entry. Now we can fill the SCP-5555 slot!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is a collaborative work with Connor MacWarren. Our preferred number slots are 5555, 5005, 5800, 5048, and 5666 in that order.

Code is at: https://github.com/not-a-seagull/5000proposal and https://github.com/not-a-seagull/not-a-seagull.github.io

CSS Help: Lambda Fairy, Uncle Nicolini

Draft Help: Sterbai , Uncle Nicolini, Cerastes, XilasCrowe, Nameless Mediocre, Dr Balthazaar, Henzoid, spaghety, Sebarus, Lt Flops, Laneous

Image Carousel: http://topia.wikidot.com/cqb:carousel

Images and videos:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:OOjs_UI_icon_printer-ltr.svg
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ojlKy5Lib4g
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Night_sky_Freycinet_2.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Crown_of_Thorns_Starfish_at_Malapascuas_Island_v._II.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Inuit_Woman_1907_Crisco_edit_2.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cheddar_Pink,_Fire_Witch_(3977452253).jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Night_sky_Freycinet_4.jpg
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Iris_-_left_eye_of_a_girl.jpg
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vuSmPshVGDA
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Old_Sardinian_Man.jpg

Edit: Given the negative reception to the news article, we have removed it.
